basic_details.job_title: ISRO Recruitment 2026: 242 Assistant, UDC, JPA & Stenographer Posts – Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), Department of Space
basic_details.total_vacancies: Assistant, Upper Division Clerk (UDC), Junior Personal Assistant (JPA), Stenographer
basic_details.job_location: Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Hassan, Hyderabad, Mahendragiri, New Delhi, Sriharikota, Thiruvananthapuram, and Autonomous Bodies
basic_details.short_information: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), Department of Space, Government of India, has invited online applications for 242 vacancies of Assistant, Upper Division Clerk (UDC), Junior Personal Assistant (JPA) and Stenographer under Advt No. ISRO:ICRB:01(A-JPA):2026. Graduates meeting the eligibility criteria can apply online at www.isro.gov.in, with computer proficiency required for all posts and stenography skills required for JPA/Stenographer posts. Posts are spread across eight ISRO centres and carry Level 4 of the Pay Matrix, with a minimum basic pay of Rs. 25,500 per month. The last d

application_fee.all_candidates_initial_payment_fee: Rs. 500/- processing fee, paid uniformly by all candidates at the time of application
application_fee.all_other_candidates_appearing_fee: Refund of Rs. 400/- (i.e., Rs. 100/- Application Fee is deducted)
application_fee.details: The processing fee is refunded only to candidates who appear in the Written Test, as per the terms above.

age_limit.details: Age relaxation, wherever posts are reserved, is also applicable to serving Government employees, Ex-Servicemen, Persons with Benchmark Disabilities, meritorious Sportspersons, Widows, Divorced women, and women judicially separated from their husbands and not remarried, as per extant Government of India orders.

qualification.details: Eligibility differs by post category, with a common requirement of computer proficiency and, for JPA/Stenographer posts, an additional stenography speed requirement. | Graduation with a minimum of 60% marks, or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, as declared by any recognized University Graduation must have been completed within the minimum stipulated duration of the course as prescribed by the University Proficiency in the use of computers | Graduation with a minimum of 60% marks or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, completed within the minimum stipulated duration of the course, OR Diploma in Commercial/Secretarial Practice with a minimum of 60% marks or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, completed within the minimum stipulated duration, plus one year of experience as Steno-Typist/Stenographer A minimum speed of 60 w.p.m. in English Shorthand/Stenography Proficiency in the use of computers | Candidates holding a Diploma in Commercial Practice (DCP) or Diploma in Commercial and Computer Practice (DCCP), along with an Apprenticeship Training Certificate issued by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (Apprenticeship Act 1961), will have this considered equivalent to one year of Steno-Typist/Stenographer experience. | Where a University's Degree Certificate or Consolidated Mark-sheet mentions both CGPA and percentage, at least one of the two criteria must meet ISRO/DOS eligibility norms. Where only CGPA is mentioned, the CGPA itself must meet ISRO/DOS eligibility criteria; conversion of CGPA to percentage is not permitted for determining eligibility, regardless of any conversion formula prescribed by the University. For Dual/Integrated Degree Programmes, if CGPA/percentage for Graduation and Post-Graduation are mentioned separately, the Graduation-level figure is reckoned. If a common CGPA/percentage is given for the entire Dual/Integrated Degree course, that combined figure is used for eligibility. Where no CGPA/percentage formula is prescribed for a Dual/Integrated Degree, the cumulative CGPA/percentage as per the Final/Consolidated Mark Sheet applies. These rules apply uniformly irrespective of whether the University prescribes a conversion formula.

salary_details.pay_scale: Level 4 of Pay Matrix (minimum basic pay Rs. 25,500 p.m.)
salary_details.details: Selected candidates for Assistant, Junior Personal Assistant, Upper Division Clerk and Stenographer posts will be placed in Level 4 of the Pay Matrix, with a minimum basic pay of Rs. 25,500 per month. In addition to basic pay, the following allowances/benefits are admissible as per extant Central Government rules: | Dearness Allowance (DA) House Rent Allowance (HRA) Transport Allowance New Pension Scheme Medical facilities for self and dependents, subsidised canteen, limited quarters facility (in lieu of HRA) Leave Travel Concession (LTC) Group Insurance House Building Advance (HBA)

selection_process: The notification confirms that shortlisted candidates will appear for a Written Test
since fee refunds are explicitly linked to Written Test appearance. Further stages of selection (such as skill test
or document verification) are not specified in this notification; candidates should refer to the detailed notification or updates on www.isro.gov.in for the complete selection process.
how_to_apply: Visit the official ISRO website: www.isro.gov.in
Navigate to the Careers/Recruitment section and locate Advt No. ISRO:ICRB:01(A-JPA):2026
Read the detailed notification carefully to confirm eligibility for the post applied for
Register/log in and complete the online application form
Upload the required documents/photograph and signature as specified in the detailed notification
Pay the applicable processing fee online
Submit the application before the last date, 16 August 2026
Save/print the confirmation page for future reference
important_instructions: Candidates should visit www.isro.gov.in for full details on posts, eligibility, and reservation points. CGPA-to-percentage conversion is not permitted for determining eligibility, even if a University prescribes a conversion formula. Diploma in Commercial Practice/Diploma in Commercial and Computer Practice candidates with a NATS/BOAT Apprenticeship Training Certificate may count it as equivalent to one year of Steno-Typist/Stenographer experience. The last date for online registration is 16 August 2026; the Government encourages candidates to apply well in advance to avoid last-minute technical issues.

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Notification Reference
content_sections.text: ISRO:ICRB:01(A-JPA):2026, dated 27-07-2026 CBC No.: 49101/11/0003/2627 Reference: EN 17/40 Official Employment News Notification

rows.details: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), Department of Space
Assistant, Upper Division Clerk (UDC), Junior Personal Assistant (JPA), Stenographer
Level 4 of Pay Matrix (minimum basic pay Rs. 25,500 p.m.)
Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Hassan, Hyderabad, Mahendragiri, New Delhi, Sriharikota, Thiruvananthapuram, and Autonomous Bodies

content_sections.heading: ISRO Assistant, UDC, JPA & Stenographer Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: Vacancies are distributed across eight ISRO Centres/Units under six post codes. Post No. 1, 3 and 5 correspond to Assistant/Upper Division Clerk category posts, while Post No. 2, 4 and 6 correspond to Junior Personal Assistant/Stenographer category posts, as per the official notification. Category-wise/post code-wise reservation points are not detailed in this notification excerpt. Candidates should check the official ISRO website, www.isro.gov.in, for the full reservation roster.

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility differs by post category, with a common requirement of computer proficiency and, for JPA/Stenographer posts, an additional stenography speed requirement. Graduation with a minimum of 60% marks, or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, as declared by any recognized University Graduation must have been completed within the minimum stipulated duration of the course as prescribed by the University Proficiency in the use of computers Graduation with a minimum of 60% marks or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, completed within the minimum stipulated duration of the course, OR Diploma in Commercial/Secretarial Practice with a minimum of 60% marks or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, completed within the minimum stipulated duration, plus one year of experience as Steno-Typist/Stenographer A minimum speed of 60 w.p.m. in English Shorthand/Stenography Proficiency in the use of computers Candidates holding a Diploma in Commercial Practice (DCP) or Diploma in Commercial and Computer Practice (DCCP), along with an Apprenticeship Training Certificate issued by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (Apprenticeship Act 1961), will have this considered equivalent to one year of Steno-Typist/Stenographer experience. Where a University's Degree Certificate or Consolidated Mark-sheet mentions both CGPA and percentage, at least one of the two criteria must meet ISRO/DOS eligibility norms. Where only CGPA is mentioned, the CGPA itself must meet ISRO/DOS eligibility criteria; conversion of CGPA to percentage is not permitted for determining eligibility, regardless of any conversion formula prescribed by the University. For Dual/Integrated Degree Programmes, if CGPA/percentage for Graduation and Post-Graduation are mentioned separately, the Graduation-level figure is reckoned. If a common CGPA/percentage is given for the entire Dual/Integrated Degree course, that combined figure is used for eligibility. Where no CGPA/percentage formula is prescribed for a Dual/Integrated Degree, the cumulative CGPA/percentage as per the Final/Consolidated Mark Sheet applies. These rules apply uniformly irrespective of whether the University prescribes a conversion formula.

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age relaxation, wherever posts are reserved, is also applicable to serving Government employees, Ex-Servicemen, Persons with Benchmark Disabilities, meritorious Sportspersons, Widows, Divorced women, and women judicially separated from their husbands and not remarried, as per extant Government of India orders.

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Salary and Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected candidates for Assistant, Junior Personal Assistant, Upper Division Clerk and Stenographer posts will be placed in Level 4 of the Pay Matrix , with a minimum basic pay of Rs. 25,500 per month . In addition to basic pay, the following allowances/benefits are admissible as per extant Central Government rules: Dearness Allowance (DA) House Rent Allowance (HRA) Transport Allowance New Pension Scheme Medical facilities for self and dependents, subsidised canteen, limited quarters facility (in lieu of HRA) Leave Travel Concession (LTC) Group Insurance House Building Advance (HBA)

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Application Fee
content_sections.text: The processing fee is refunded only to candidates who appear in the Written Test, as per the terms above.

rows.fee_structure: Rs. 500/- processing fee, paid uniformly by all candidates at the time of application

rows.category: Women / SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen (exempted categories, appearing in Written Test)
All other candidates (appearing in Written Test)
rows.fee_structure: Refund of Rs. 400/- (i.e., Rs. 100/- Application Fee is deducted)

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: The notification confirms that shortlisted candidates will appear for a Written Test , since fee refunds are explicitly linked to Written Test appearance. Further stages of selection (such as skill test, interview, or document verification) are not specified in this notification; candidates should refer to the detailed notification or updates on www.isro.gov.in for the complete selection process.

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Required Documents
content_sections.text: The list of documents required at the time of application or Written Test is not specified in this notification. Candidates are generally advised to keep the following ready, subject to confirmation from the detailed ISRO notification: Educational certificates and mark-sheets/consolidated mark-sheet Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen), if applicable Age proof Recent passport-size photograph and signature for online upload Valid ID proof

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for ISRO Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official ISRO website: www.isro.gov.in Navigate to the Careers/Recruitment section and locate Advt No. ISRO:ICRB:01(A-JPA):2026 Read the detailed notification carefully to confirm eligibility for the post applied for Register/log in and complete the online application form Upload the required documents/photograph and signature as specified in the detailed notification Pay the applicable processing fee online Submit the application before the last date, 16 August 2026 Save/print the confirmation page for future reference

content_sections.heading: ISRO Recruitment 2026 Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates should visit www.isro.gov.in for full details on posts, eligibility, and reservation points. CGPA-to-percentage conversion is not permitted for determining eligibility, even if a University prescribes a conversion formula. Diploma in Commercial Practice/Diploma in Commercial and Computer Practice candidates with a NATS/BOAT Apprenticeship Training Certificate may count it as equivalent to one year of Steno-Typist/Stenographer experience. The last date for online registration is 16 August 2026; the Government encourages candidates to apply well in advance to avoid last-minute technical issues.

content_sections.text: Employment News Notification PDF : Click here Official Website: Click here Join Arattai Channel: Click Here Join Telegram Channel: Click Here Join WhatsApp Channel : Click Here Sarkari Result : Click Here Download Mobile App: Click Here Candidates who have completed their graduation can also explore more Graduate-Level Government Jobs and Central Government Jobs 2026 available across India. Applicants specifically interested in office and administrative roles can check the latest Assistant and UDC Jobs and Stenographer Jobs 2026 matching their qualification and typing/stenography skills.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in ISRO Recruitment 2026 for Assistant, UDC, JPA and Stenographer posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 242 vacancies across Assistant, Upper Division Clerk, Junior Personal Assistant, and Stenographer posts under Advt No. ISRO:ICRB:01(A-JPA):2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for ISRO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online registration is 16 August 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification for ISRO Assistant and UDC posts?
faq.answer: Graduation with a minimum of 60% marks or CGPA of 6.32 on a 10-point scale, completed within the minimum stipulated course duration, along with proficiency in computers.

faq.question: Q4. What is the required stenography speed for ISRO JPA/Stenographer posts?
faq.answer: A minimum speed of 60 words per minute in English Shorthand/Stenography is required, in addition to graduation or an equivalent diploma with relevant experience.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for ISRO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 28 years as on 16-08-2026, with relaxation to 31 years for OBC and 33 years for SC/ST candidates, wherever posts are reserved.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for ISRO Assistant, UDC, JPA and Stenographer posts?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be placed in Level 4 of the Pay Matrix with a minimum basic pay of Rs. 25,500 per month, plus DA, HRA, Transport Allowance and other Central Government benefits.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for ISRO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: All candidates initially pay a processing fee of Rs. 500/-, of which Rs. 100/- is the actual application fee. The remaining amount is refunded to candidates who appear in the Written Test, with full refund for women, SC/ST, PwBD and Ex-Servicemen candidates.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for ISRO Recruitment 2026 online?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official ISRO website, www.isro.gov.in, before the last date, 16 August 2026.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for ISRO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The notification confirms a Written Test as part of the selection process. Further stages are not specified in this notification and candidates should check the detailed notification on the official website.
